A key property of a BST is that all values to the left are smaller and all values to the right are larger than the current node.
If both p and q are smaller than the current root's value, then the LCA must be in the left subtree.
If both p and q are larger than the current root's value, then the LCA must be in the right subtree.
If p and q lie on opposite sides (or one of them is equal to the current root), then the current root IS the LCA.
